::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(here)
library(janitor)
library(knitr)
library(readxl)
```
:::



# Introduction

Replace this paragraph with a short introduction. State your research question,
your outcome, your main exposure, and why a busy public health or clinical
reader should care.

Default question:

> Among patients in the QCRC COVID-19 ICU dataset, what patient characteristics
> and clinical factors are associated with 30-day mortality?

# Methods

## Data source

The analysis used the `Main_Dataset` sheet from the deidentified QCRC COVID-19
ICU workbook included with the course materials.



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
to_numeric <- function(x) {
  as.numeric(dplyr::na_if(as.character(x), "."))
}

read_qcrc_main <- function(path = here::here("data", "QCRC_FINAL_Deidentified.xlsx")) {
  readxl::read_xlsx(path, sheet = "Main_Dataset") |>
    janitor::clean_names() |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      bmi = to_numeric(bmi),
      crp = to_numeric(crp),
      d_dimer = to_numeric(d_dimer),
      vent_los = to_numeric(vent_los),
      x30d_mortality = factor(
        x30d_mortality,
        levels = c(0, 1),
        labels = c("No 30-day mortality", "30-day mortality")
      ),
      died = factor(died, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Survived", "Died")),
      intubated = factor(intubated, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
      remdesivir_or_placebo = factor(
        remdesivir_or_placebo,
        levels = c(0, 1),
        labels = c("Placebo", "Remdesivir")
      ),
      female = factor(female),
      race = factor(race)
    )
}

qcrc <- read_qcrc_main()
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
data.frame(
  rows = nrow(qcrc),
  columns = ncol(qcrc)
) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Basic dimensions of the QCRC analysis dataset.")
```

## Variables

Replace this paragraph with your outcome, exposure, and adjustment variables.
For the default report, the outcome is 30-day mortality. Candidate exposures
include age, BMI, treatment group, intubation, CRP, or D dimer.



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
analysis_vars <- c(
  "x30d_mortality",
  "age",
  "bmi",
  "female",
  "intubated",
  "remdesivir_or_placebo"
)

missing_counts <- sapply(qcrc[analysis_vars], \(x) sum(is.na(x)))

data.frame(
  variable = names(missing_counts),
  missing = as.integer(missing_counts)
) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Missing values for the planned analysis variables.")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


Table: Missing values for the planned analysis variables.

|variable              | missing|
|:---------------------|-------:|
|x30d_mortality        |       0|
|age                   |       0|
|bmi                   |       3|
|female                |       0|
|intubated             |       0|
|remdesivir_or_placebo |       0|


:::
:::



## Statistical analysis

Replace this paragraph with your analysis plan. Use cautious language such as
"we estimated associations" unless your design supports causal claims.

# Results

## Descriptive results



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
qcrc |>
  dplyr::group_by(x30d_mortality) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n = dplyr::n(),
    mean_age = round(mean(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    mean_bmi = round(mean(bmi, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    intubated_percent = round(mean(intubated == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Selected QCRC characteristics by 30-day mortality status.")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


Table: Selected QCRC characteristics by 30-day mortality status.

|x30d_mortality      |   n| mean_age| mean_bmi| intubated_percent|
|:-------------------|---:|--------:|--------:|-----------------:|
|No 30-day mortality | 194|     60.1|     33.1|              68.6|
|30-day mortality    |  94|     70.4|     28.5|              84.0|


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(qcrc, aes(x = x30d_mortality, y = bmi)) +
  geom_boxplot(na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "BMI"
  )
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![BMI distribution by 30-day mortality status in the QCRC dataset.](qcrc-mini-manuscript-starter_files/figure-html/bmi-by-mortality-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



## First-pass model



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
model_30d <- glm(
  x30d_mortality ~ age + bmi + intubated + remdesivir_or_placebo,
  data = qcrc,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model_30d)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Call:
glm(formula = x30d_mortality ~ age + bmi + intubated + remdesivir_or_placebo, 
    family = binomial, data = qcrc)

Coefficients:
                                Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)                     -2.31314    1.19892  -1.929  0.05369 .  
age                              0.04436    0.01114   3.983 6.81e-05 ***
bmi                             -0.07458    0.02301  -3.242  0.00119 ** 
intubatedYes                     1.46234    0.37355   3.915 9.05e-05 ***
remdesivir_or_placeboRemdesivir -1.04033    0.41672  -2.496  0.01254 *  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)

    Null deviance: 361.41  on 284  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance: 300.08  on 280  degrees of freedom
  (3 observations deleted due to missingness)
AIC: 310.08

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 5
```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
or_table <- data.frame(
  term = names(coef(model_30d)),
  odds_ratio = exp(coef(model_30d)),
  exp(confint.default(model_30d)),
  check.names = FALSE
)

names(or_table) <- c("term", "odds_ratio", "conf_low", "conf_high")

or_table |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(where(is.numeric), \(x) round(x, 2))
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Adjusted odds ratios from a first-pass logistic regression.")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


Table: Adjusted odds ratios from a first-pass logistic regression.

|                                |term                            | odds_ratio| conf_low| conf_high|
|:-------------------------------|:-------------------------------|----------:|--------:|---------:|
|(Intercept)                     |(Intercept)                     |       0.10|     0.01|      1.04|
|age                             |age                             |       1.05|     1.02|      1.07|
|bmi                             |bmi                             |       0.93|     0.89|      0.97|
|intubatedYes                    |intubatedYes                    |       4.32|     2.08|      8.98|
|remdesivir_or_placeboRemdesivir |remdesivir_or_placeboRemdesivir |       0.35|     0.16|      0.80|


:::
:::
